investment_views.logic_chain: Anthropic bet heavily on coding as the breakout use case, which serves as the gateway into enterprise IT budgets. This strategy has driven massive ARR growth and enabled product extensions like Co-work and Computer Use, making them the dominant player in enterprise AI.

evidence.evidence_detail: Added $6 billion in annual run rate in February alone; launched Opus 4.6, Claude Code, Co-work, and Computer Use.

investment_views.risk_warning: Anthropic's push for a Washington permissioning regime (regulatory capture) could create heavy-handed barriers that stifle broader industry innovation.
investment_views.source_quote: $6 billion in annual run rate was added in February alone... The company made a big bet on coding as the kind of big breakout use case... code is the gateway into enterprise and enterprise IT budgets.

investment_views.logic_chain: OpenAI and Anthropic have fundamentally different business models and revenue recognition methods. OpenAI is 75% consumer subscriptions, while Anthropic is mostly enterprise/API. Direct ARR comparisons are misleading; OpenAI remains the overwhelming revenue generator but Anthropic is catching up in enterprise.

evidence.evidence_detail: OpenAI is 3/4 consumer subscriptions and 1/4 API; Anthropic is almost the exact opposite. Revenue recognition differs (conservative vs gross tonnage).

investment_views.risk_warning: Media narratives pitting the two against each other based on unnormalized ARR numbers create false equivalencies.
investment_views.source_quote: Open AI is 3/4 consumer subscriptions and a quarter API. Anthropic is almost the exact opposite... they're two totally different conversations... if you normalize these two businesses, what you would see is Open AI is still the overwhelming revenue generator.

investment_views.logic_chain: OpenAI's consumer market share is declining as major tech players (Apple, Meta, Microsoft) integrate AI natively at the OS level. In response, OpenAI is cutting side projects like Sora to refocus on core enterprise and consumer offerings.

evidence.evidence_detail: Consumer market share dropped from 100% in 2023 to 85% in 2024, and 75% in 2025. Sora video app shut down, cancelling a $1B Disney licensing deal.

investment_views.risk_warning: Apple, Meta, and Windows intercepting consumer queries natively could permanently cap OpenAI's consumer TAM.
investment_views.source_quote: Drop down to 85% market share 2024, 75% market share 2025... Apple, Meta, and obviously Windows... Sora video app. That's been shut down... Disney was going to put a billion dollars into OpenAI... All of that's now been cancelled.

investment_views.logic_chain: The prospect of superintelligence increases discount rates and terminal disruption risk. The market is re-rating SaaS stocks from P/E to FCF multiples, questioning the long-term durability of their cash flows in a world of constant AI-driven disruption.

evidence.evidence_detail: Snowflake's FCF payback period dropped from nearly 100 years in 2023 to about 50 years. ServiceNow, Atlassian, Workday seeing similar multiple compression.

investment_views.risk_warning: If superintelligence arrives, traditional software moats may be obliterated, making long-term terminal value assumptions invalid.
investment_views.source_quote: Snowflake, in 2023, it would have taken you almost 100 years. And where is it now? It's been cut in half... If super intelligence is coming, we have to be very careful about what we're willing to pay for these things.

investment_views.logic_chain: The market views Mag 7 cash flows as monopolistically durable forever, expanding their multiples. Conversely, NVIDIA is treated like a SaaS company with questioned long-term durability despite massive revenue and margins, indicating a complex market reset regarding AI infrastructure vs. platform moats.

evidence.evidence_detail: NVIDIA making $200 billion with highest margins, but market treats its durability like ServiceNow/Snowflake, while Mag 7 multiples expand.

investment_views.risk_warning: Market skepticism about the long-term durability of hardware/semiconductor cash flows compared to software/platform monopolies.
investment_views.source_quote: Apple, Microsoft, Meta, and Alphabet... we believe that these cash flows are essentially monopolistically durable forever... except Nvidia. Which is the most unbelievably accretive... making $200 billion. And they're treating it like they're treating ServiceNow and Snowflake.

investment_views.logic_chain: Consumers will pay premium subscriptions for high-value AI meta-services (booking travel, managing email), creating a new platform ecosystem where advertisers pay to be embedded, similar to the mobile app economy.

evidence.evidence_detail: Spotify has 290M paid subs, Netflix 325M. AI could command $80-$100/month subscriptions and become the new platform layer.

investment_views.risk_warning: Apple, Google, and Meta may give away base AI queries for free, supported by ads, taking revenue oxygen away from standalone subscription models.
investment_views.source_quote: Spotify... 290 million subscribers... Netflix has 325 million... AI that can book your travel... is likely going to be the most valuable meta service... consumer AI app becomes the new platform, much like the iPhone

investment_views.logic_chain: In an era of AI-driven digital abundance, traditional brand pricing power erodes. Consumers prioritize cheaper, faster, better products. True moats shift to network effects, physical assets, and hard tech manufacturing.

evidence.evidence_detail: Tesla vs BMW/BYD, Apple launching $600 MacBook Neo. LVMH/Ferrari pricing power eroding as abundance beats brand affiliation.

investment_views.risk_warning: Companies relying solely on brand premium without operational or technological superiority will face severe margin compression.
investment_views.source_quote: brands go to zero... when you can make things that are as good or better and you can make them in a cheaper, faster, better way, people want that abundance more than they wanted affiliation to a brand.

tech_industry_insights.insight: Anthropic is pursuing a 'regulatory capture' strategy by lobbying for a Washington permissioning regime for chips and models, which would create heavy-handed barriers protecting incumbents.
tech_industry_insights.source_quote: They do want a permissioning regime in Washington for chips and models... I do think it is a form of regulatory capture because it plays into the hands of the big companies and creates motes that new entrants will not be able to overcome.

tech_industry_insights.insight: Private Equity firms (e.g., Thoma Bravo) are executing AI roll-ups, acquiring traditional businesses like accounting and healthcare firms to apply AI change management and drive operational efficiency.
tech_industry_insights.source_quote: big VC kind of starting to look like private equity, buying out hospitals, accounting firms... putting them all together and then running them with AI... They're kind of betting on the idea that they can own the change management around AI.

tech_industry_insights.insight: Perplexity's 'Model Council' feature highlights the value of agnostic wrappers that compare multiple LLMs, showing that routing and comparison can be a strong product differentiator without owning the base model.
tech_industry_insights.source_quote: The model council is like the greatest feature... It will go to all three different major models... tells you where they differ and it tries to figure out why they differ.

tech_industry_insights.insight: Google is launching Workspace Studio for AI automation, leveraging its existing access to user data (calendar, email, documents) to build trusted enterprise agents without requiring users to share data with new startups.
tech_industry_insights.source_quote: Google has announced Google workspace studio to do AI automation... they already have access to your calendar, your documents, your email. So, the agent doesn't really have to earn your trust

risks.description: Regulatory Capture Risk: Anthropic lobbying for heavy-handed AI regulation in Washington could stifle open-source and startup innovation.
risks.source_quote: They do want a permissioning regime in Washington for chips and models... it is a form of regulatory capture

risks.description: Valuation Compression Risk: The theoretical threat of superintelligence is causing markets to increase discount rates and compress terminal multiples for SaaS companies.
risks.source_quote: If super intelligence is coming, we have to be very careful about what we're willing to pay for these things... all the discount rates are going through the roof.

risks.description: Consumer Market Share Risk: OpenAI is losing consumer query share to native OS integrations by Apple, Meta, and Microsoft.
risks.source_quote: Apple, Meta, and obviously Windows... starting to intercept... ChatGPT is going to have some big challenges there on the consumer side.

tracking_signals.trigger_condition: Monitor OpenAI's revenue mix shift towards enterprise and the fallout from cancelling consumer side-projects like Sora.
tracking_signals.source_quote: new found focus on chasing Anthropic down the enterprise path... cutting back on all their side projects.

tracking_signals.trigger_condition: Watch for the rollout and enterprise adoption of Google's 'open claw' agent leveraging native Workspace data.
tracking_signals.source_quote: waiting for the Google version of open claw because I don't really want to share all my documents with some new service.

tracking_signals.trigger_condition: Track the continued divergence in Free Cash Flow payback multiples between Mag 7 and traditional SaaS stocks.
tracking_signals.source_quote: market is completely flipped the other way... re-rating everything down... changing the way that things are being framed from price to equity to a multiple of the cash that you have on hand.

key_quotes: code is the gateway into enterprise and enterprise IT budgets.
Open AI is 3/4 consumer subscriptions and a quarter API. Anthropic is almost the exact opposite... they're two totally different conversations.
If super intelligence is coming, we have to be very careful about what we're willing to pay for these things.
Apple, Microsoft, Meta, and Alphabet... we believe that these cash flows are essentially monopolistically durable forever... except Nvidia.
when you can make things that are as good or better and you can make them in a cheaper, faster, better way, people want that abundance more than they wanted affiliation to a brand.
uncertain_items: Whether consumer AI queries will ultimately be free/ad-supported (Google/Meta route) or paid subscriptions (Spotify/Netflix route).
The exact terminal value and discount rate to apply to businesses in a post-superintelligence world.
non_focus_items: California politics, state budget issues, and David Friedberg joking about running for governor.
David Sacks' role in the Trump administration and political disputes over Pentagon contracts.
Political hit pieces, Biden administration mentions, and partisan culture wars in Silicon Valley.
